title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: If you or someone you know has been diagnosed with high cholesterol, it's important to keep it under control. This video demonstrates a few ways to help you manage your cholesterol, such as diet, exercise, and medication.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: If your cholesterol levels are too high, it's important to eat a heart-healthy diet. To eat a heart-healthy diet, you need to understand how the types of fats you eat can affect your cholesterol level.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: For example, saturated fats, hydrogenated fats, and trans fats tend to raise the bad cholesterol, also called low-density lipoprotein or LDL cholesterol in your blood. These fats are found in meat and dairy products, tropical oils, and many processed foods, such as cakes and cookies.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Facing saturated fats with unsaturated fats may help lower the bad cholesterol in your blood when eaten as part of a heart-healthy diet. Unsaturated fats include polyunsaturated and mono-unsaturated fats and are found in foods such as salmon, avocados, nuts, and olive oils.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Other heart-healthy lifestyle decisions you can make are to stop smoking and get adequate, moderate intensity aerobic exercise. Walking, biking, or swimming 30 minutes a day, five days a week, may be a good exercise

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Talk to your doctor about the type of diet and exercise plans most appropriate for your health and cholesterol levels.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: If lifestyle changes can't reduce your cholesterol levels enough, your doctor may prescribe certain medications to reduce it.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Modest medicines are the most common medication for lowering LDL or bad cholesterol by reducing the amount made in your liver. However, they have modest effects on lowering another type of fat in your blood called triglycerides, and raising good cholesterol, also called high-density lipoprotein or HDL cholesterol.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Your liver uses cholesterol to make a substance called bile, which helps digest your food. Cholesterol acid binding drugs bind to bile and prevent it from being used in digestion. This causes the liver to use more cholesterol to make more bile, thereby decreasing the amount of cholesterol in your bloodstream.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Cholesterol absorption inhibitors block some of the cholesterol taken up by your intestines from the food you eat.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Fibrates are mainly used to reduce triglyceride levels in your blood, but they can also raise HDL levels.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Cholesterol acid reduces LDL cholesterol and triglycerides and increases HDL cholesterol made in your liver.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
text_en: Healthy lifestyle habits are important in controlling your cholesterol levels. If your healthcare provider prescribed medication to treat your high cholesterol, it is important to take it as directed and contact him or her if you have any questions.
